---
title: "wbc population"
output: html_document
date: "2025-11-13"
---

```{r, echo=FALSE}
 library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
Blood_count <- read_excel("smears_analysis_r_updated14.xlsx")
```


```{r, echo=FALSE}
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    `total_cell` = `[Cells] c/ml`
  )

```


```{r, echo=FALSE}
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    `Shark_Id` = `Sharks Id`
  )
```



```{r, echo=FALSE}
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    total_count        = `total count`
  )
```


```{r}
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  mutate(
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe),
    week_factor = factor(week),
    Shark_Id_factor = factor(Shark_Id),
    Sex_factor = Sex
    
  )

```
```{r}
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>% 
  filter(!is.na(Shark_Id))
```


```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# First, ensure columns are correctly renamed (if not already)
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    Lympho_wbc    = `Lympho_wbc%`,
    GT_wbc        = `GT_wbc%`,
    FE_wbc        = `FE_wbc%`,
    CE_wbc        = `CE_wbc%`,
    Neutro_wbc    = `Neutro_wbc%`,
    Monocyte_wbc  = `Monocyte_wbc%`,
    Basophil_wbc  = `basophil_wbc%`
)
```
```{r}
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    abnormal       = `[abnormal]`
  )

```


```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(car) # for Levene’s test

# --- 1. Filter only week 4 data ---
week4_data <- Blood_count %>%
  filter(week_factor == 4) %>%
  dplyr::select(Groupe_factor, Lympho_wbc, GT_wbc, CE_wbc, FE_wbc, 
                Neutro_wbc, Monocyte_wbc, Basophil_wbc)

# --- 2. Loop and store results ---
leuko_cols <- c("Lympho_wbc", "GT_wbc", "CE_wbc", "FE_wbc", 
                "Neutro_wbc", "Monocyte_wbc", "Basophil_wbc")

results_summary <- data.frame(
  Variable = leuko_cols,
  Levene_p = NA,
  SW_Control_p = NA,
  SW_Winter_p = NA,
  SW_Summer_p = NA,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

for (i in seq_along(leuko_cols)) {
  col <- leuko_cols[i]
  
  # Prepare data
  temp <- week4_data %>%
    dplyr::select(Groupe_factor, all_of(col)) %>%
    drop_na()
  
  # --- Levene’s test for variance homogeneity ---
  levene_result <- car::leveneTest(temp[[col]] ~ temp$Groupe_factor)
  results_summary[i, "Levene_p"] <- levene_result$`Pr(>F)`[1]
  
  # --- Normality test for each group ---
  shapiro_results <- by(temp[[col]], temp$Groupe_factor, shapiro.test)
  
  results_summary[i, "SW_Control_p"] <- shapiro_results$Control$p.value
  results_summary[i, "SW_Winter_p"] <- shapiro_results$Winter$p.value
  results_summary[i, "SW_Summer_p"] <- shapiro_results$Summer$p.value
}

# --- 3. Print the Summary Table ---
print(results_summary)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(car) # for Levene’s test
week4_data <- Blood_count %>%
  filter(week_factor == 6) %>%
  dplyr::select(Groupe_factor, Lympho_wbc, GT_wbc, CE_wbc, FE_wbc, 
                Neutro_wbc, Monocyte_wbc, Basophil_wbc)

# --- 2. Loop and store results ---
leuko_cols <- c("Lympho_wbc", "GT_wbc", "CE_wbc", "FE_wbc", 
                "Neutro_wbc", "Monocyte_wbc", "Basophil_wbc")

results_summary <- data.frame(
  Variable = leuko_cols,
  Levene_p = NA,
  SW_Control_p = NA,
  SW_Winter_p = NA,
  SW_Summer_p = NA,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

for (i in seq_along(leuko_cols)) {
  col <- leuko_cols[i]
  
  # Prepare data
  temp <- week4_data %>%
    dplyr::select(Groupe_factor, all_of(col)) %>%
    drop_na()
  
  # --- Levene’s test for variance homogeneity ---
  levene_result <- car::leveneTest(temp[[col]] ~ temp$Groupe_factor)
  results_summary[i, "Levene_p"] <- levene_result$`Pr(>F)`[1]
  
  # --- Normality test for each group ---
  shapiro_results <- by(temp[[col]], temp$Groupe_factor, shapiro.test)
  
  results_summary[i, "SW_Control_p"] <- shapiro_results$Control$p.value
  results_summary[i, "SW_Winter_p"] <- shapiro_results$Winter$p.value
  results_summary[i, "SW_Summer_p"] <- shapiro_results$Summer$p.value
}

# --- 3. Print the Summary Table ---
print(results_summary)
```


```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(car) # for Levene’s test

week4_data <- Blood_count %>%
  filter(week_factor == 8) %>%
  dplyr::select(Groupe_factor, Lympho_wbc, GT_wbc, CE_wbc, FE_wbc, 
                Neutro_wbc, Monocyte_wbc, Basophil_wbc)

# --- 2. Loop and store results ---
leuko_cols <- c("Lympho_wbc", "GT_wbc", "CE_wbc", "FE_wbc", 
                "Neutro_wbc", "Monocyte_wbc", "Basophil_wbc")

results_summary <- data.frame(
  Variable = leuko_cols,
  Levene_p = NA,
  SW_Control_p = NA,
  SW_Winter_p = NA,
  SW_Summer_p = NA,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

for (i in seq_along(leuko_cols)) {
  col <- leuko_cols[i]
  
  # Prepare data
  temp <- week4_data %>%
    dplyr::select(Groupe_factor, all_of(col)) %>%
    drop_na()
  
  # --- Levene’s test for variance homogeneity ---
  levene_result <- car::leveneTest(temp[[col]] ~ temp$Groupe_factor)
  results_summary[i, "Levene_p"] <- levene_result$`Pr(>F)`[1]
  
  # --- Normality test for each group ---
  shapiro_results <- by(temp[[col]], temp$Groupe_factor, shapiro.test)
  
  results_summary[i, "SW_Control_p"] <- shapiro_results$Control$p.value
  results_summary[i, "SW_Winter_p"] <- shapiro_results$Winter$p.value
  results_summary[i, "SW_Summer_p"] <- shapiro_results$Summer$p.value
}

# --- 3. Print the Summary Table ---
print(results_summary)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(car)

# Filter the data to include ONLY Week 4
week4_data <- Blood_count %>%
  filter(week_factor == 4) %>%
  # Drop columns not needed for this simple ANOVA if they cause issues
  dplyr::select(Groupe_factor, Monocyte_wbc) %>% 
  drop_na()

# --- Assumption Check Reminder ---
# We run these to confirm Monocytes meet the requirements for ANOVA:

# A. Homogeneity of Variances (Levene's Test)
# P-value must be > 0.05
levene_check <- car::leveneTest(Monocyte_wbc ~ Groupe_factor, data = week4_data)
print("Levene's Test for Homogeneity of Variance:")
print(levene_check)

# B. Normality Check (Shapiro-Wilk for each group)
# P-values must be > 0.05 for each group
print("Shapiro-Wilk Normality Test by Group:")
print(by(week4_data$Monocyte_wbc, week4_data$Groupe_factor, shapiro.test))

# Only run this if the ANOVA P-value for Groupe_factor was < 0.05
print("Tukey's HSD Post-Hoc Test:")
TukeyHSD(monocyte_anova_wk4)
```
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
# Load rstatix for easy group-wise statistical testing
# install.packages("rstatix")
library(rstatix) 

# --- Filter and select data ---
analysis_data <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::select(Groupe_factor, week_factor, Shark_Id, Monocyte_wbc) %>%
  drop_na(Monocyte_wbc)

# --- Run One-Way ANOVA on each Week separately ---
anova_results <- analysis_data %>%
  group_by(week_factor) %>%
  anova_test(Monocyte_wbc ~ Groupe_factor) %>%
  # Adjust P-values for the three tests (Wk4, Wk6, Wk8) using Bonferroni
  adjust_pvalue(method = "bonferroni") %>% 
  add_significance("p.adj")

print("1. ANOVA (Treatment) Results for each Week:")
print(anova_results)

# --- Post-Hoc Test (if any ANOVA is significant, P.adj < 0.05) ---
# If a specific week's ANOVA is significant, you must run Tukey's HSD:

post_hoc_wk4 <- analysis_data %>%
  filter(week_factor == 4) %>%
  tukey_hsd(Monocyte_wbc ~ Groupe_factor) %>%
  add_significance()

print("2. Post-Hoc Tukey HSD for Week 4 (Run only if ANOVA was significant):")
print(post_hoc_wk4)
# Repeat for Week 6 and Week 8 if their respective ANOVAs were significant.
```
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
# Load rstatix for easy group-wise statistical testing
# install.packages("rstatix")
library(rstatix) 

# --- Filter and select data ---
analysis_data <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::select(Groupe_factor, week_factor, Shark_Id, Monocyte_wbc) %>%
  drop_na(Monocyte_wbc)

# --- Run One-Way ANOVA on each Week separately ---
anova_results <- analysis_data %>%
  group_by(week_factor) %>%
  anova_test(Monocyte_wbc ~ Groupe_factor) %>%
  # Adjust P-values for the three tests (Wk4, Wk6, Wk8) using Bonferroni
  adjust_pvalue(method = "bonferroni") %>% 
  add_significance("p.adj")

print("1. ANOVA (Treatment) Results for each Week:")
print(anova_results)

# --- Post-Hoc Test (if any ANOVA is significant, P.adj < 0.05) ---
# If a specific week's ANOVA is significant, you must run Tukey's HSD:

post_hoc_wk6 <- analysis_data %>%
  filter(week_factor == 6) %>%
  tukey_hsd(Monocyte_wbc ~ Groupe_factor) %>%
  add_significance()

print("2. Post-Hoc Tukey HSD for Week 6 (Run only if ANOVA was significant):")
print(post_hoc_wk6)
# Repeat for Week 6 and Week 8 if their respective ANOVAs were significant.
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(rstatix) 

# --- Filter and select data ---
analysis_data <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::select(Groupe_factor, week_factor, Shark_Id, Monocyte_wbc) %>%
  drop_na(Monocyte_wbc)

# --- Run One-Way ANOVA on each Week separately ---
anova_results <- analysis_data %>%
  group_by(week_factor) %>%
  anova_test(Monocyte_wbc ~ Groupe_factor) %>%
  # Adjust P-values for the three tests (Wk4, Wk6, Wk8) using Bonferroni
  adjust_pvalue(method = "bonferroni") %>% 
  add_significance("p.adj")

print("1. ANOVA (Treatment) Results for each Week:")
print(anova_results)

# --- Post-Hoc Test (if any ANOVA is significant, P.adj < 0.05) ---
# If a specific week's ANOVA is significant, you must run Tukey's HSD:

post_hoc_wk8 <- analysis_data %>%
  filter(week_factor == 8) %>%
  tukey_hsd(Monocyte_wbc ~ Groupe_factor) %>%
  add_significance()

print("2. Post-Hoc Tukey HSD for Week 8 (Run only if ANOVA was significant):")
print(post_hoc_wk8)
# Repeat for Week 6 and Week 8 if their respective ANOVAs were significant.
```
```{r}
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)

# --- 1. Rank Transformation ---
# Apply a rank transformation to the CE data across all observations
Blood_count$CE_rank <- rank(Blood_count$CE_wbc)

lmm_ce_rank <- lmer(
  CE_rank ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
  data = Blood_count
)
print("--- ANOVA on Rank-Transformed CE Data (Impact of Time and Group) ---")
anova_result <- anova(lmm_ce_rank)
print(anova_result)


print("\n--- Non-Parametric Week Comparisons (Within Groups) ---")
emms_ce_rank <- emmeans(lmm_ce_rank, specs = ~ week_factor | Groupe_factor)

# Pairwise comparisons of weeks within each treatment group
pairs_ce_rank <- pairs(emms_ce_rank, adjust = "bonferroni")
print(pairs_ce_rank)

library(DHARMa)


print("--- DHARMa Residual Diagnostics for CE Rank-LMM ---")

res_ce_rank <- simulateResiduals(
  fittedModel = lmm_ce_rank, 
  n = 1000, 
  plot = TRUE
)

# 1. Check for Uniformity (Overall Fit)
# P-value must be >= 0.05
testUniformity(res_ce_rank) 

# 2. Check for Dispersion (Over/Under-dispersion)
# P-value must be >= 0.05
testDispersion(res_ce_rank) 

# 3. Check for Zero-Inflation (Was the ranking effective?)
# P-value must be >= 0.05
testZeroInflation(res_ce_rank)
testQuantiles(res_ce_rank, plot = TRUE)
library(emmeans)

# Assuming 'lmm_ce_rank' is the LMM fitted to the CE_rank variable

# 1. Calculate Estimated Marginal Means (EMMs) for the Groupe_factor, 
#    averaging across the levels of week_factor.
emms_ce_group <- emmeans(lmm_ce_rank, specs = ~ Groupe_factor)

print("--- Estimated Marginal Means (Ranks) for Treatment Groups ---")
print(emms_ce_group)

# 2. Perform all pairwise comparisons between groups using the Tukey adjustment.
pairs_ce_group <- pairs(emms_ce_group, adjust = "tukey")

print("\n--- Pairwise Comparison of Treatment Groups (Control vs. Summer vs. Winter) ---")
print(pairs_ce_group)
library(emmeans)
emms_ce_week_group <- emmeans(lmm_ce_rank, specs = ~ Groupe_factor | week_factor)
summary(lmm_ce_rank)
# Perform pairwise comparisons using Tukey adjustment
pairs_ce_week_group <- pairs(emms_ce_week_group, adjust = "tukey")

# Print the results to the console for a quick check
print("--- Pairwise Comparisons of Groups WITHIN Each Week (CE Rank) ---")
print(pairs_ce_week_group)

# --- 2. Prepare Data for Excel Export ---

# Convert the emmeans pairs object to a data frame
pairs_ce_df <- as.data.frame(pairs_ce_week_group)

# Separate the 'contrast' column into clean Group/Week columns for better Excel readability
pairs_ce_df_export <- pairs_ce_df %>%
  # Separate the original contrast (e.g., 'Control - Summer')
  separate(contrast, into = c("Group1", "Group2"), sep = " - ", remove = TRUE) %>%
  # Clean up column names and add significance flags
  mutate(
    # The 'week_factor' column already contains the week the comparison was performed at.
    Direction = ifelse(estimate > 0, paste(Group1, ">", Group2), paste(Group2, ">", Group1)),
    Significant = ifelse(p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No")
  ) %>%
  # Select and reorder columns for a clean Excel table
  select(
    week_factor, Group1, Group2, estimate, SE, df, t.ratio, p.value, Significant, Direction
  ) %>%
  rename(
      # Rename 'week_factor' to 'Week' for clarity
      Week = week_factor,
      `P-Value` = p.value,
      Estimate = estimate
  )

# --- 3. Export to Excel ---
write_xlsx(pairs_ce_df_export, "CE_Within_Week_Group_Comparisons.xlsx")

print("\nSuccessfully created and exported: CE_Within_Week_Group_Comparisons.xlsx")

```
```{r}
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)
library(DHARMa)

# --- 1. Rank Transformation ---
# Apply a rank transformation to the Monocyte data across all observations
Blood_count$Monocyte_rank <- rank(Blood_count$Monocyte_wbc)

# --- 2. Fit LMM to the Ranks ---
# We use the LMM on ranks to handle unbalanced data (N changing over time).
lmm_monocyte_rank <- lmer(
  Monocyte_rank ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
  data = Blood_count
)

# --- 3. Overall ANOVA (Effect of Time and Group) ---
print("--- ANOVA on Rank-Transformed Monocyte Data ---")
anova_result_monocyte <- anova(lmm_monocyte_rank)
print(anova_result_monocyte)

# --- 4. Diagnostics Check (CRUCIAL) ---
print("\n--- DHARMa Residual Diagnostics ---")
res_monocyte_rank <- simulateResiduals(
  fittedModel = lmm_monocyte_rank, 
  n = 1000, 
  plot = TRUE
)

testUniformity(res_monocyte_rank)
testDispersion(res_monocyte_rank) 
testZeroInflation(res_monocyte_rank)

# --- 5. Post-Hoc Analysis (Within-Group Week Comparison) ---
print("\n--- Non-Parametric Week Comparisons (Within Groups) ---")
emms_monocyte_rank <- emmeans(lmm_monocyte_rank, specs = ~ week_factor | Groupe_factor)

# Pairwise comparisons of weeks within each treatment group
pairs_monocyte_rank <- pairs(emms_monocyte_rank, adjust = "bonferroni")
print(pairs_monocyte_rank)

emms_monocyte_week_group <- emmeans(lmm_monocyte_rank, specs = ~ Groupe_factor | week_factor)

overall_groups_monocyte <- pairs(emmeans(lmm_monocyte, ~ Groupe_factor), adjust = "tukey")
overall_weeks_monocyte <- pairs(emmeans(lmm_monocyte, ~ week_factor), adjust = "tukey")

# Pairwise comparisons of groups within each level of week_factor (Tukey adjustment)
pairs_monocyte_week_group <- pairs(emms_monocyte_week_group, adjust = "tukey")

print("--- Pairwise Comparison of Treatment Groups WITHIN Each Week ---")
print(pairs_monocyte_week_group)

summary(lmm_monocyte)
# --- B. Prepare for Excel Export ---

pairs_monocyte_df <- as.data.frame(pairs_monocyte_week_group)

pairs_monocyte_df_export <- pairs_monocyte_df %>%
  # Separate the original contrast (e.g., 'Control - Summer')
  separate(contrast, into = c("Group1", "Group2"), sep = " - ", remove = TRUE) %>%
  # Add significance flags
  mutate(
    Direction = ifelse(estimate > 0, paste(Group1, ">", Group2), paste(Group2, ">", Group1)),
    Significant = ifelse(p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No")
  ) %>%
  # Select and reorder columns
  select(
    week_factor, Group1, Group2, estimate, SE, df, t.ratio, p.value, Significant, Direction
  ) %>%
  rename(
      Week = week_factor,
      `P-Value` = p.value,
      Estimate = estimate
  )

write_xlsx(pairs_monocyte_df_export, "Monocyte_Within_Week_Group_Comparisons.xlsx")
print("\nSuccessfully exported: Monocyte_Within_Week_Group_Comparisons.xlsx")

```

```{r}
```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# --- 1. Calculate Summary Statistics (Raw Data) ---
monocyte_summary <- Blood_count %>%
  # Group by the factors you want to plot
  group_by(Sex_factor, Groupe_factor, week_factor) %>%
  summarise(
    # Calculate Mean Monocyte percentage
    mean_monocyte = mean(Monocyte_wbc, na.rm = TRUE),
    # Calculate Standard Error (SE = SD / sqrt(n))
    se_monocyte = sd(Monocyte_wbc, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    n_obs = n(),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# --- 2. Create the Faceted Plot ---

# (Assuming 'custom_colors' is defined from previous steps: 
# custom_colors <- c("Control" = "#009E73", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Summer" = "#D55"))
custom_colors <- c(
  "Control" = "#C8C0A9",
  "Winter" = "#3182BD",
  "Summer" = "#D97A66"
)
p_monocyte_faceted <- ggplot(
  monocyte_summary, 
  aes(x = week_factor, y = mean_monocyte, fill = Groupe_factor)
) +
  # Bar chart for the mean
  geom_bar(
    stat = "identity", 
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), 
    color = "black"
  ) +
  # Error bars for the Standard Error
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = mean_monocyte - se_monocyte, ymax = mean_monocyte + se_monocyte),
    width = 0.2, 
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), 
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  # Apply the custom colors
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  
  # --- CRITICAL: Facet by Sex_factor ---
  facet_wrap(
    ~ Sex_factor, 
    scales = "fixed" # Ensures the Y-axis scale is the same for both plots
  ) +
  
  # Labels and Theme
  labs(
    title = "Monocyte Percentage Across Weeks and Groups, Split by Sex",
    x = "Week",
    y = "Mean Monocyte (%) ± SE",
    fill = "Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = "black"),
        panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines")) # Add space between facets

print(p_monocyte_faceted)
```



```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
custom_colors <- c(
  "Control" = "#C8C0A9",
  "Winter" = "#3182BD",
  "Summer" = "#D97A66"
)
# Ensure all factors are correctly set up (Groupe_factor, week_factor, Sex_factor)
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  mutate(
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe),
    week_factor = factor(week),
    Sex_factor = factor(Sex)
  )

# --- 1. Calculate Summary Statistics (Raw Data) ---
ce_summary <- Blood_count %>%
  # Group by the factors you want to plot
  group_by(Sex_factor, Groupe_factor, week_factor) %>%
  summarise(
    # Calculate Mean CE percentage
    mean_ce = mean(CE_wbc, na.rm = TRUE),
    # Calculate Standard Error (SE = SD / sqrt(n))
    se_ce = sd(CE_wbc, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    n_obs = n(),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# --- 2. Create the Faceted Plot ---

# Note: The 'custom_colors' vector must be defined in your R session.

p_ce_faceted <- ggplot(
  ce_summary, 
  aes(x = week_factor, y = mean_ce, fill = Groupe_factor)
) +
  # Bar chart for the mean
  geom_bar(
    stat = "identity", 
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), 
    color = "black"
  ) +
  # Error bars for the Standard Error
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = mean_ce - se_ce, ymax = mean_ce + se_ce),
    width = 0.2, 
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), 
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  # Apply the custom colors
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  
  # --- CRITICAL: Facet by Sex_factor ---
  facet_wrap(
    ~ Sex_factor, 
    scales = "fixed" # Ensures the Y-axis scale is the same for both plots
  ) +
  
  # Labels and Theme
  labs(
    title = "CE Percentage Across Weeks and Groups, Split by Sex",
    x = "Week",
    y = "Mean CE (%) ± SE",
    fill = "Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = "black"),
        panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines"))

print(p_ce_faceted)
```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Ensure all factors are correctly set up (Groupe_factor, week_factor, Sex_factor)
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  mutate(
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe),
    week_factor = factor(week),
    Sex_factor = factor(Sex)
  )

# --- 1. Calculate Summary Statistics (Raw Data) ---
lympho_summary <- Blood_count %>%
  # Group by the factors you want to plot
  group_by(Sex_factor, Groupe_factor, week_factor) %>%
  summarise(
    # Calculate Mean Lymphocyte percentage
    mean_lympho = mean(Lympho_wbc, na.rm = TRUE),
    # Calculate Standard Error (SE = SD / sqrt(n))
    se_lympho = sd(Lympho_wbc, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    n_obs = n(),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# --- 2. Create the Faceted Plot ---

# Note: The 'custom_colors' vector must be defined in your R session.

p_lympho_faceted <- ggplot(
  lympho_summary, 
  aes(x = week_factor, y = mean_lympho, fill = Groupe_factor)
) +
  # Bar chart for the mean
  geom_bar(
    stat = "identity", 
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), 
    color = "black"
  ) +
  # Error bars for the Standard Error
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = mean_lympho - se_lympho, ymax = mean_lympho + se_lympho),
    width = 0.2, 
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), 
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  # Apply the custom colors
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  
  # --- CRITICAL: Facet by Sex_factor ---
  facet_wrap(
    ~ Sex_factor, 
    scales = "fixed" # Ensures the Y-axis scale is the same for both plots
  ) +
  
  # Labels and Theme
  labs(
    title = "Lymphocyte Percentage Across Weeks and Groups, Split by Sex",
    x = "Week",
    y = "Mean Lymphocyte (%) ± SE",
    fill = "Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = "black"),
        panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines"))

print(p_lympho_faceted)
```

```{r}
library(lme4)
library(dplyr)
library(stats)

# --- 0. Data Preparation (Create Rank Variables) ---

# Create rank columns for Monocyte and CE
Blood_count$Monocyte_wbc_rank <- rank(Blood_count$Monocyte_wbc)
Blood_count$CE_wbc_rank <- rank(Blood_count$CE_wbc)

# Ensure all necessary factors are defined
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  mutate(
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe),
    week_factor = factor(week),
    Sex_factor = factor(Sex),
    Tank_factor = factor(Tank),
    Shark_Id_factor = factor(Shark_Id)
  )

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# A. MONOCYTE COMPARISON
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------

print("--- MONOCYTE (Rank) Model Comparison: Tank and Sex Factors ---")

# Model A (Reduced - No Sex, No Tank)
model_monocyte_A <- lmer(
  Monocyte_wbc_rank ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
  data = Blood_count, REML = FALSE
) 

# Model C (Reduced + Tank - No Sex, Added Tank Factor)
model_monocyte_C <- lmer(
  Monocyte_wbc_rank ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor) + (1 | Tank_factor),
  data = Blood_count, REML = FALSE
)

# Model D (Full + Tank - With Sex, Added Tank Factor)
model_monocyte_D <- lmer(
  Monocyte_wbc_rank ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor) + (1 | Tank_factor),
  data = Blood_count, REML = FALSE
)

results_monocyte <- data.frame(
  Variable = "Monocyte",
  Model = c("A (Best Reduced)", "C (Reduced + Tank)", "D (Full + Tank)"),
  AIC = c(AIC(model_monocyte_A), AIC(model_monocyte_C), AIC(model_monocyte_D)),
  BIC = c(BIC(model_monocyte_A), BIC(model_monocyte_C), BIC(model_monocyte_D)),
  LogLik = c(logLik(model_monocyte_A), logLik(model_monocyte_C), logLik(model_monocyte_D)),
  df = c(attr(logLik(model_monocyte_A), "df"), attr(logLik(model_monocyte_C), "df"), attr(logLik(model_monocyte_D), "df"))
)

results_monocyte <- results_monocyte %>% arrange(AIC)
print(results_monocyte)

cat("\n", rep("-", 60), "\n\n")

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# B. CE COMPARISON
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------

print("--- CE (Rank) Model Comparison: Tank and Sex Factors ---")

# Model A (Reduced - No Sex, No Tank)
model_ce_A <- lmer(
  CE_wbc_rank ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
  data = Blood_count, REML = FALSE
) 

# Model C (Reduced + Tank - No Sex, Added Tank Factor)
model_ce_C <- lmer(
  CE_wbc_rank ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor) + (1 | Tank_factor),
  data = Blood_count, REML = FALSE
)

# Model D (Full + Tank - With Sex, Added Tank Factor)
model_ce_D <- lmer(
  CE_wbc_rank ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor) + (1 | Tank_factor),
  data = Blood_count, REML = FALSE
)

results_ce <- data.frame(
  Variable = "CE",
  Model = c("A (Best Reduced)", "C (Reduced + Tank)", "D (Full + Tank)"),
  AIC = c(AIC(model_ce_A), AIC(model_ce_C), AIC(model_ce_D)),
  BIC = c(BIC(model_ce_A), BIC(model_ce_C), BIC(model_ce_D)),
  LogLik = c(logLik(model_ce_A), logLik(model_ce_C), logLik(model_ce_D)),
  df = c(attr(logLik(model_ce_A), "df"), attr(logLik(model_ce_C), "df"), attr(logLik(model_ce_D), "df"))
)

results_ce <- results_ce %>% arrange(AIC)
print(results_ce)
```
```{r}

```

```{r}
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)
library(DHARMa) 
library(dplyr)
library(writexl) # CRITICAL: For exporting to Excel

# --- 0. Data Preparation and Static Fix ---

# CRITICAL FIX 1: Ensure the base column 'Groupe' uses "Static" instead of "Contrôle"
Blood_count$Groupe[Blood_count$Groupe == "Contrôle"] <- "Static"

Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  mutate(
    week_factor = factor(week),
    Groupe_factor = factor(
      Groupe, 
      levels = c("Winter", "Static", "Summer") # Ensure final order
    ),
    Sex_factor = factor(Sex),
    Shark_Id_factor = factor(Shark_Id)
  )

# --- 1. Fit LMM using the RAW variable (Neutrophil_wbc) ---
lmm_neutrophil_raw <- lmer(Neutro_wbc ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
                           data = Blood_count)
summary(lmm_neutrophil_raw)
print(lmm_neutrophil_raw)
anova(lmm_neutrophil_raw)

# --- 2. Model Validation (DHARMa Residuals) ---
# Validation is still good practice even if the variable is raw
simulationOutput_neutrophil <- simulateResiduals(lmm_neutrophil_raw)
testUniformity(simulationOutput_neutrophil)
testDispersion(simulationOutput_neutrophil)
testOutliers(simulationOutput_neutrophil)

# --- 3. Post-hoc comparisons: Groupe × Week ---
emms_neutrophil_raw <- emmeans(lmm_neutrophil_raw, ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor)
pairs_neutrophil_raw <- pairs(emms_neutrophil_raw, adjust = "tukey")

# Convert to data frame for export
pairs_neutrophil_df <- as.data.frame(pairs_neutrophil_raw) 

# --- 4. Post-hoc comparisons: Sex within each Groupe × Week ---
emms_sex_neutrophil_raw <- emmeans(lmm_neutrophil_raw, ~ Sex_factor | Groupe_factor * week_factor)
pairs_sex_neutrophil_raw <- pairs(emms_sex_neutrophil_raw, adjust = "tukey")

# Convert to data frame for export
pairs_sex_df <- as.data.frame(pairs_sex_neutrophil_raw)

# --- 5. EXPORT RESULTS TO EXCEL ---
write_xlsx(
  list(
    "Groupe_x_Week_Pairs" = pairs_neutrophil_df,
    "Sex_within_GxW_Pairs" = pairs_sex_df
  ), 
  path = "Neutrophil_LMM_Results.xlsx"
)

# Print summaries for immediate review
print(summary(lmm_neutrophil_raw))
print(summary(pairs_neutrophil_raw))
print(summary(pairs_sex_neutrophil_raw))
```


```{r}
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    immature = `[immature]`,
    mitosis   = `[mitosis]`,
    abnormal  = `[abnormal]`
  )
```
```{r}
# Shapiro tests
shapiro.test(Blood_count$immature)
shapiro.test(Blood_count$mitosis)
shapiro.test(Blood_count$abnormal)

# Levene tests
leveneTest(Blood_count$immature ~ Groupe_factor, Blood_count)
leveneTest(Blood_count$mitosis ~ Groupe_factor, Blood_count)
leveneTest(Blood_count$abnormal ~ Groupe_factor, Blood_count)

```
```{R}
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)
library(DHARMa)
library(glmmTMB)
library(writexl)

Blood_count$Groupe[Blood_count$Groupe == "Control"] <- "Static"
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  mutate(     # rename if your column is `rbc:wbc`
    week_factor = factor(week),
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe),
    Sex_factor = factor(Sex),
    Shark_Id_factor = factor(Shark_Id) # replace with your shark ID column name
  )

# --- 1. Rank Transformation ---
# Apply a rank transformation to the Monocyte data across all observations
Blood_count$Immature_rank <- rank(Blood_count$immature)

# --- 2. Fit LMM to the Ranks ---
# We use the LMM on ranks to handle unbalanced data (N changing over time).
lmm_Immature_rank <-  glmmTMB(
  immature ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor), # Conditional (Count) Model
  ziformula = ~ 1, # Zero-Inflation Model: Intercept-only (constant zero-inflation probability)
  data = Blood_count,
  family = nbinom2 # Negative Binomial Type 2 distribution
)



# --- 4. Diagnostics Check (CRUCIAL) ---
print("\n--- DHARMa Residual Diagnostics ---")
res_immature_rank <- simulateResiduals(
  fittedModel = lmm_Immature_rank, 
  n = 1000, 
  plot = TRUE
)

testUniformity(res_immature_rank)
testDispersion(res_immature_rank) 
testZeroInflation(res_immature_rank)

print(lmm_Immature_rank)
summary(lmm_Immature_rank)
# --- 5. Post-Hoc Analysis (Within-Group Week Comparison) ---
print("\n--- Non-Parametric Week Comparisons (Within Groups) ---")
emms_immature_rank <- emmeans(lmm_Immature_rank, specs = ~ week_factor | Groupe_factor)

# Pairwise comparisons of weeks within each treatment group
pairs_immature_rank <- pairs(emms_immature_rank , adjust = "bonferroni")
print(pairs_immature_rank)

emms_immature_week_group <- emmeans(lmm_Immature_rank, specs  = ~ Groupe_factor | week_factor)

overall_groups_immature <- pairs(emmeans(lmm_Immature_rank, ~ Groupe_factor), adjust = "tukey")
overall_weeks_immature <- pairs(emmeans(lmm_Immature_rank, ~ week_factor), adjust = "tukey")

# Pairwise comparisons of groups within each level of week_factor (Tukey adjustment)
pairs_immature_week_group <- pairs(emms_immature_week_group, adjust = "tukey")

print("--- Pairwise Comparison of Treatment Groups WITHIN Each Week ---")
print(pairs_immature_week_group)
pairs_df <- as.data.frame(pairs_immature_week_group)
output_path <- "Immature_Group_Comparisons.xlsx"

# Write the data frame to the Excel file
write_xlsx(
  x = pairs_df,
  path = output_path
)

print(paste("Successfully exported to:", output_path))
```
```{r}
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)
library(DHARMa)
library(glmmTMB)
library(writexl)


Blood_count$Groupe[Blood_count$Groupe == "Control"] <- "Static"
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  mutate(     # rename if your column is `rbc:wbc`
    week_factor = factor(week),
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe),
    Sex_factor = factor(Sex),
    Shark_Id_factor = factor(Shark_Id),
    mitosis = `[mitosis]`
  )

# NOTE: The data frame 'Blood_count' must contain the 'mitosis' variable
# and all required factor columns (Groupe_factor, week_factor, Sex_factor, Shark_Id_factor).

# --- 1. Fit ZINB GLMM for Mitosis Counts ---
# Fitting the Zero-Inflated Negative Binomial model to count data.
print("--- Fitting Mitosis GLMM (Zero-Inflated Negative Binomial) ---")
lmm_mitosis_zinb <- glmmTMB(
  mitosis ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor), # Conditional (Count) Model
  ziformula = ~ 1, # Zero-Inflation Model: Intercept-only
  data = Blood_count,
  family = nbinom2 # Negative Binomial Type 2 distribution
)

print(summary(lmm_mitosis_zinb))

# --- 2. Diagnostics Check (CRUCIAL) ---
print("\n--- DHARMa Residual Diagnostics for Mitosis Model ---")
# Simulating residuals to check model fit
res_mitosis_zinb <- simulateResiduals(
  fittedModel = lmm_mitosis_zinb, 
  n = 500, # Use a reasonable number of simulations
  plot = TRUE # Plots the diagnostic figures
)

testUniformity(res_mitosis_zinb)
testDispersion(res_mitosis_zinb) 
testZeroInflation(res_mitosis_zinb)


# --- 3. Post-Hoc Analysis - Two Interaction Slices ---

# a) Comparison of WEEKS within each treatment GROUP (e.g., Control w4 vs Control w6)
# This addresses your request for 'contorl 4 to control 6' type comparisons.
print("\n--- Pairwise Comparison of WEEKS WITHIN Each Treatment Group (Bonferroni) ---")
emms_mitosis_weeks_within_groups <- emmeans(lmm_mitosis_zinb, specs = ~ week_factor | Groupe_factor)
pairs_mitosis_weeks_within_groups <- pairs(emms_mitosis_weeks_within_groups , adjust = "bonferroni")
print(pairs_mitosis_weeks_within_groups)


# b) Comparison of GROUPS within each WEEK (e.g., Control w4 vs TreatmentA w4)
# This addresses your request for comparisons of groups 'across time' (at specific time points).
print("\n--- Pairwise Comparison of GROUPS WITHIN Each Week (Tukey) ---")
emms_mitosis_groups_within_weeks <- emmeans(lmm_mitosis_zinb, specs  = ~ Groupe_factor | week_factor)
pairs_mitosis_groups_within_weeks <- pairs(emms_mitosis_groups_within_weeks, adjust = "tukey")
print(pairs_mitosis_groups_within_weeks)


# --- 4. Export to Single Excel File with Multiple Sheets using writexl ---

# Convert both emmeans objects into standard R data frames
weeks_within_groups_df <- as.data.frame(pairs_mitosis_weeks_within_groups)
groups_within_weeks_df <- as.data.frame(pairs_mitosis_groups_within_weeks)


# Create a NAMED LIST of data frames. The names become the sheet names in the Excel file.
list_to_export <- list(
  "Weeks_Within_Groups" = weeks_within_groups_df, # Sheet 1: Time trends in each treatment
  "Groups_Within_Weeks" = groups_within_weeks_df  # Sheet 2: Treatment differences at each time point
)


# Define the output path
output_path <- "Mitosis_PostHoc_Comparisons.xlsx"

# Write the list of data frames to the Excel file
write_xlsx(
  x = list_to_export,
  path = output_path
)

print(paste("\nSuccessfully exported two pairwise comparison tables to a single Excel file:", output_path))
print("The file contains two sheets: 'Weeks_Within_Groups' and 'Groups_Within_Weeks'")
```

```{r}
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)
library(writexl)
Blood_count$Groupe[Blood_count$Groupe == "Control"] <- "Static"
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  mutate(     # rename if your column is `rbc:wbc`
    week_factor = factor(week),
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe),
    Sex_factor = factor(Sex),
    Shark_Id_factor = factor(Shark_Id) # replace with your shark ID column name
  )

# NOTE: The data frame 'Blood_count' must contain the 'abnormal' variable
# and all required factor columns (Groupe_factor, week_factor, Sex_factor, Shark_Id_factor).

# --- 1. Create Ranked Variable ---
print("--- Creating Ranked Variable for 'abnormal' ---")
# Creates a new column 'abnormal_rank' based on the overall rank of 'abnormal' scores.
Blood_count$abnormal_rank <- rank(Blood_count$abnormal, ties.method = "average")


# --- 2. Fit LMM to the Ranks ---
# We fit a standard Linear Mixed Model (LMM) to the ranked data.
print("\n--- Fitting LMM to Ranks of 'abnormal' ---")
lmm_abnormal_rank <- lmer(
  abnormal_rank ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor), 
  data = Blood_count
)

print(summary(lmm_abnormal_rank))
print(anova(lmm_abnormal_rank)) # ANOVA for testing main effects and interaction


# --- 3. Post-Hoc Analysis - Two Interaction Slices ---
# Post-hoc tests are performed on the LMM object using emmeans.
# Results are interpreted based on the mean ranks.

# a) Comparison of WEEKS within each treatment GROUP
print("\n--- Pairwise Comparison of WEEKS WITHIN Each Treatment Group (Bonferroni) ---")
emms_abnormal_rank_weeks_within_groups <- emmeans(lmm_abnormal_rank, specs = ~ week_factor | Groupe_factor)
pairs_abnormal_rank_weeks_within_groups <- pairs(emms_abnormal_rank_weeks_within_groups, adjust = "bonferroni")
print(pairs_abnormal_rank_weeks_within_groups)


# b) Comparison of GROUPS within each WEEK
print("\n--- Pairwise Comparison of GROUPS WITHIN Each Week (Tukey) ---")
emms_abnormal_rank_groups_within_weeks <- emmeans(lmm_abnormal_rank, specs  = ~ Groupe_factor | week_factor)
pairs_abnormal_rank_groups_within_weeks <- pairs(emms_abnormal_rank_groups_within_weeks, adjust = "tukey")
print(pairs_abnormal_rank_groups_within_weeks)
# --- 3. Diagnostics Check (CRUCIAL) ---
print("\n--- DHARMa Residual Diagnostics for LMM on Ranks ---")
# Simulating residuals to check model fit on the rank scale
# NOTE: DHARMa is designed for GLMMs, but works well for LMMs to check for
# homogeneity and outliers. We treat the LMM as a conditional model here.
res_abnormal_rank <- simulateResiduals(
  fittedModel = lmm_abnormal_rank, 
  n = 500, # Use a reasonable number of simulations
  plot = TRUE # Plots the diagnostic figures
)
testUniformity(res_abnormal_rank) 
# Test for over/under-dispersion (less relevant for LMM on ranks, but good to run)
testDispersion(res_abnormal_rank)
# --- 4. Export to Single Excel File with Multiple Sheets using writexl ---

# Convert both emmeans objects into standard R data frames
weeks_within_groups_rank_df <- as.data.frame(pairs_abnormal_rank_weeks_within_groups)
groups_within_weeks_rank_df <- as.data.frame(pairs_abnormal_rank_groups_within_weeks)


# Create a NAMED LIST of data frames.
list_to_export <- list(
  "Rank_Weeks_Within_Groups" = weeks_within_groups_rank_df, 
  "Rank_Groups_Within_Weeks" = groups_within_weeks_rank_df  
)


# Define the output path
output_path <- "Abnormal_Rank_PostHoc_Comparisons.xlsx"

# Write the list of data frames to the Excel file
write_xlsx(
  x = list_to_export,
  path = output_path
)

print(paste("\nSuccessfully exported two pairwise comparison tables to a single Excel file:", output_path))
print("The file contains two sheets: 'Rank_Weeks_Within_Groups' and 'Rank_Groups_Within_Weeks'")
```
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(rstatix)
library(writexl)

# --- 1. Filter Data for Week 4 ---
Blood_count_W4 <- Blood_count %>%
  filter(week_factor == "4")

# --- 2. Kruskal-Wallis Test across Groups at Week 4 ---
# Test for an overall difference in medians among the groups.
kruskal_W4_groups <- Blood_count_W4 %>%
  kruskal_test(mitosis ~ Groupe_factor)

# --- 3. Post-hoc Pairwise Wilcoxon Tests for Group Differences ---
# Test all pairs of groups, correcting for multiple comparisons (using Benjamini-Hochberg).
pairwise_W4_groups <- Blood_count_W4 %>%
  pairwise_wilcox_test(
    mitosis ~ Groupe_factor,
    p.adjust.method = "BH", 
    detailed = TRUE # Shows full statistics, including T and P values
  )

# --- 4. Print Results ---
print("--- Kruskal-Wallis Test (Overall Group Difference at Week 4) ---")
print(kruskal_W4_groups)
print("\n--- Pairwise Wilcoxon Tests (Specific Group Differences at Week 4) ---")
print(pairwise_W4_groups)

# --- 5. Export to Excel (Optional but Recommended) ---
# Creates a list for export
list_to_export <- list(
  "Kruskal_Wallis_W4" = as.data.frame(kruskal_W4_groups), 
  "Pairwise_Wilcox_W4" = as.data.frame(pairwise_W4_groups)
)

output_path <- "Mitotic_W4_Group_Differences.xlsx"
write_xlsx(x = list_to_export, path = output_path)

print(paste("\nSuccessfully exported results to:", output_path))
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(rstatix) 

# Filter data for Week 4
Blood_count_W4 <- Blood_count %>%
  filter(week_factor == "4")

# Kruskal-Wallis test across groups for week 4 (Overall test)
kruskal_W4_groups <- Blood_count_W4 %>%
  kruskal_test(mitosis ~ Groupe_factor)
print(kruskal_W4_groups)

# Post-hoc pairwise Wilcoxon tests to see difference statistics between groups
pairwise_W4_groups <- Blood_count_W4 %>%
  pairwise_wilcox_test(
    mitosis ~ Groupe_factor,
    p.adjust.method = "BH"
  )
print(pairwise_W4_groups)
```
